step_by_step_thinking:
The blood supply for the scaphoid does indeed primarily come from the dorsal branch of the radial artery, which provides an arterial network that runs along its length. However, fractures involving the distal pole can disrupt this vascular supply more significantly due to their proximity to where it becomes less redundant. This disruption leads to increased risk of nonunion and subsequent avascular necrosis compared to mid-shaft or proximal pole fractures.
